---
title: ""
subtitle: 'The title "Data Scientist" arrived in 2025, but the craft was honed long before. The work behind it started long before with building clustering models for fraud detection during internal audit engagements, developing ARIMA and SARIMAX revenue forecasts within finance departments, and engineering a Python-based purchase-to-payment monitoring application that replaced traditional audit cycles years before I pursued a formal degree.'
image: assets/images/profile-pic.jpg
image-alt: "Mohamed Bakr profile photo"
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 14em
  id: hero-heading
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mohdbakr/
      aria-label: "LinkedIn logo linking to Mohamed Bakr account"
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener noreferrer
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/Mohdbakr
      aria-label: "GitHub logo linking to Mohamed Bakr account"
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener noreferrer
    - text: '{{< fa envelope title="Contact" >}} Contact Me'
      href: /contact.html
      aria-label: "Contact logo linking to Mohamed Bakr contact page"
      
page-layout: full
---

::: {#hero-heading}
<div class="d-flex flex-column align-items-center">
![](assets/logos/logo_with_name_light.svg){.author-logo .light-content}
![](assets/logos/logo_with_name_dark.svg){.author-logo .dark-content}

<p class="about-subtitle" style="color: var(--bs-secondary); font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.08em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; text-align: center;">CIA · CFE · DATA SCIENTIST</p>
</div>



::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
The [Berkeley MIDS](https://ischoolonline.berkeley.edu/data-science/curriculum/){target="_blank"} from the [School of Information](https://www.ischool.berkeley.edu/){target="_blank"} provided the formal mathematical foundations for my work. I now integrate this academic rigor with my background as a FP&A manager, CIA and CFE to build ML, GenAI, and statistical inference solutions for clients.

Arabic calligraphy is my parallel practice a discipline that demands the same focus: finding precise, geometric structure within apparent freedom.
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
[!['Those truly fear Allah, among His Servants, who have knowledge' — Fatir:28](assets/images/Mohamed-Bakr-Arabic-Calligraphy.png){fig-alt="A professional close-up photograph of Mohamed Bakr in profile, deeply focused on performing traditional Arabic calligraphy with a reed pen (Qalam) and black ink. He is writing the Quranic verse: 'إنما يخشى الله من عباده العلماء' ('Those truly fear Allah, among His Servants, who have knowledge' — Fatir:28) in Ruq'ah script. The photo also features a traditional ink well positioned on the parchment." .about-image}](https://www.instagram.com/mbakr84/){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
::: 

:::
::: 

## What I Do

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

The problems I find most compelling are those where domain fluency dictates the model’s architecture, not just its tuning. In fraud examination, understanding the mechanics of a disbursement scheme determines which features must be engineered. In FP&A, knowing how a budget is structurally assembled determines which residuals actually deserve attention.

I build fast, test adversarially, and refuse to deploy until I understand what a model has actually learned—beyond just passing its performance metrics. This habit is a direct inheritance from the world of audit: a clean reconciliation is not just a goal; it is a rigorous test of whether you are capable of catching a well-hidden fraud.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
In the earlier chapters of my career, I built end-to-end solutions that spans from data ingestion, EDA, cleaning, descriptive modeling, and board-level reporting to evaluate large-scale capital projects and pinpoint the drivers of variance. The job title was "Audit," but the work was data science before it had a name.

I operate most effectively at the intersection of domain expertise and quantitative rigor — where the business problem shapes the equation, and the equation refines the question.
:::

:::

## Education & Credentials

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### {{< fa graduation-cap >}} Education

- **M.S. in Information and Data Science**
  *UC Berkeley, USA* · 2025
- **B.Sc. of Commerce, Accounting**
  *Alexandria University, Egypt* · 2004

::: {.cert-badges .edu-badges}
[![UC Berkeley](assets/images/credentials/ucb.png)](https://www.ischool.berkeley.edu/){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
[![Alexandria University](assets/images/credentials/alexandria.png)](https://alexu.edu.eg){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### {{< fa certificate >}} Certifications

- **Certified Internal Auditor (CIA)** · *The IIA*
- **Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE)** · *ACFE*

::: {.cert-badges}
[![Certified Internal Auditor](assets/images/credentials/cia.png)](https://www.theiia.org){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
[![Certified Fraud Examiner](assets/images/credentials/cfe.png)](https://www.acfe.com){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
:::
:::

:::

## Updates

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Projects
::: {#project}
:::
[See all &rarr;](projects.html){.about-links .subtitle}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Blog
::: {#blog}
:::
[See all &rarr;](posts/index.html){.about-links .subtitle}
:::

::::
